sections.lead: Foundation models struggle to build autonomous AI agents because they lack specific organizational context, leading to inaccurate results.

bullets.label: Brick Wall
bullets.text: Agents hit a "brick wall" because they lack internal context to produce accurate, actionable results.

bullets.label: Specific Knowledge
bullets.text: They fail to understand tasks like how to compute "weekly active users" from proprietary data.

bullets.label: Scattered Context
bullets.text: Vital corporate knowledge is scattered across metadata catalogs, wikis, code comments, or engineers' heads.

bullets.label: Solve From Scratch
bullets.text: Developers are forced to repeatedly solve context assembly from scratch for each new project.

sections.lead: Developers are addressing the AI context problem by using shared markdown libraries as living wikis, leveraging LLMs for management.

bullets.label: Markdown Libraries
bullets.text: Developers provide AI agents with shared markdown libraries that act as living wikis.

bullets.label: LLM Strength
bullets.text: This approach leverages LLMs' strength in tedious bookkeeping and cross-referencing tasks.

bullets.label: Bespoke Implementations
bullets.text: Current implementations are often bespoke and siloed, preventing knowledge sharing across teams.

bullets.label: OKF Need
bullets.text: An open standard like OKF is needed for interoperable knowledge sharing across all teams and vendors.

sections.lead: Open Knowledge Format (OKF) version 0.1 is proposed as a vendor-neutral specification to formalize the LLM Wiki pattern.

bullets.label: Vendor Neutral
bullets.text: OKF version 0.1 is proposed as a vendor-neutral specification to solve knowledge fragmentation.

bullets.label: Portable Interoperable
bullets.text: It formalizes the LLM Wiki pattern into a portable, interoperable format specifically for AI metadata and context.

bullets.label: Version Control
bullets.text: OKF knowledge can live in version control alongside code, effortlessly moving between systems.

bullets.label: Simple Format
bullets.text: It consists of markdown files with YAML front matter, requiring no complex compression, new runtimes, or SDKs.

sections.lead: An OKF bundle structures knowledge with lightweight YAML front matter, where the file path defines the concept's identity.

bullets.label: YAML Front Matter
bullets.text: An OKF bundle is structured with lightweight YAML front matter at the top of an OKF document.

bullets.label: Type Field
bullets.text: The `type` field is the only required element, defining the concept, such as a BigQuery table or metric.

bullets.label: File Path Identity
bullets.text: The file path itself acts as the concept's identity, and directories form rich graphs.

bullets.label: Markdown Links
bullets.text: Standard markdown links connect concepts, allowing a metric to link to its underlying database table.

bullets.label: Index Log
bullets.text: Bundles can also include `index.md` for navigation and `log.md` for history tracking.

sections.lead: OKF is built on three core design principles: minimal opinionation, independent producer/consumer, and being a format, not a platform.

bullets.label: Minimally Opinionated
bullets.text: OKF is minimally opinionated, requiring only a `type` field, leaving content structure to the knowledge producer.

bullets.label: Producer Independence
bullets.text: It ensures producer and consumer independence, acting as a clean, standardized contract between parties.

bullets.label: Format Not Platform
bullets.text: OKF is a format, not a platform, not tied to any proprietary cloud, database, or agent framework.

bullets.label: Tooling Swaps
bullets.text: This design allows for independent tooling swaps for both knowledge writers and readers, like AI agents.

sections.lead: To get started with OKF, read the V0.1 spec, write producers and consumers, and explore Google Cloud's GitHub proofs of concept.

bullets.label: Google PoC
bullets.text: Google Cloud has provided proofs of concept on GitHub, including a BigQuery enrichment agent and an HTML graph visualizer.

bullets.label: Sample Bundles
bullets.text: Sample OKF bundles are available for GA4, Stack Overflow, and Bitcoin to demonstrate its use.

bullets.label: Read V0.1 Spec
bullets.text: The first step is to read the V0.1 spec, which is presented as a single page document.

bullets.label: Write Producer
bullets.text: Users should write a producer for their source system to generate OKF knowledge.

bullets.label: Write Consumer
bullets.text: Users should also write a consumer, which could be a viewer, search index, or an AI agent.

bullets.label: Contribute
bullets.text: Users can contribute to the open standard by filing issues or proposing extensions.

sections.lead: OKF empowers AI agents by providing a standard, interoperable way to understand internal organizational knowledge.

bullets.label: Overcome Fragmentation
bullets.text: OKF enables AI systems to overcome context fragmentation within an organization.

bullets.label: Eliminate Busy Work
bullets.text: This approach eliminates tedious human busy work related to context assembly.

bullets.label: Unlock Capabilities
bullets.text: It unlocks new capabilities for building intelligent AI systems that understand proprietary data.

bullets.label: Explore GitHub
bullets.text: Users are encouraged to explore the GitHub repo and experiment with OKF to see its impact.
